properties.remark: HORSE SHED COMPLETELY BLOWN OVER

1 TO 2 MILES WEST OF SPIRITWOOD...RAINFALL TOTALS MEASURED 2 TO 3 INCHES. WATER WAS FLOWING OVER A TOWNSHIP ROAD 2 MILES WEST OF SPIRITWOOD. ALSO 4 MILES NORTHEAST OF JAMESTOWN 2 FEET OF WATER WAS FLOWING OVER A COUNTY ROAD.
HAIL ONGOING AT TIME OF REPORT. LITTLE WIND. HEAVY RAIN.

NUMEROUS ROADS FLOODED AND WASHED OUT. THIS IS MAINLY BOUNDED BY SPIRITWOOD LAKE TO THE NORTH AND INTERSTATE 94 TO THE SOUTH...JAMESTOWN TO THE WEST AND THE BARNES COUNTY LINE TO THE EAST. RAIN REPORTS OF 4 INCHES OVER THE PAST 4 HOURS. RECORD RAIN AT THE JAMESTOWN AIRPORT FOR MAY 23 OF AT LEAST 2.95 INCHES...STILL RAINING.
WATER FLOWING OVER HIGHWAY 49 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 23 AND 24. TIME ESTIMATED. DELAYED REPORT.
HAIL COVERED THE GROUND WHITE. ALSO HAD NEARLY AN INCH OF RAIN. NOT MUCH WIND.
QUARTER HAIL AND EXTENSIVE DAMAGE REPORTED ALONG THE MONTANA...NORTH DAKOTA BORDER.
HAIL BEGAN 543 PM AND CONTINUED AT TIME OF THIS REPORT ISSUANCE. VERY HEAVY RAIN. NOT MUCH WIND.
THERE IS A PHOTO OF THIS STONE ON NWS BISMARCK FACEBOOK PAGE. HAIL WAS SOFTER AND TRAINED SPOTTER SAID HE WAS UNSURE OF DAMAGE TO VEHICLES BUT DID NOT SEE ANY BROKEN WINDOWS.

REPORT WAS FROM AROUND BUFORD. PERSON DROVE TOWARD TRENTON AND IT WAS HAILING ALONG THE ROUTE. LARGEST STONES ONE INCH DIAMETER. MOST HAIL WAS THREE-QUARTERS INCH IN DIAMETER. HAIL COVERING GROUND TO ABOUT 70 PERCENT COVERAGE.
HAIL LASTED UNTIL ABOUT 610 PM. NO DAMAGE REPORTED.
A LOT OF PEA SIZE HAIL. NOT MANY GOLF BALL SIZE STONES. HAIL LASTED UNTIL ABOUT 630 PM TO 635 PM. 0.30 INCHES OF RAIN. MANY LEAVES KNOCKED OFF OF TREES.
HAIL LASTED A FEW MINUTES AT MOST.
MCKENZIE COUNTY DEPUTY SHERIFF REPORTED HAIL BETWEEN GOLF BALL AND BASEBALL SIZE UNTIL 720 PM CDT.
TORNADO WAS REPORTED ALONG HIGHWAY 85 AT 740 PM CDT. MOVING SOUTHEAST.

*** 9 INJ *** EF2 TORNADO 5 MILES SOUTH OF WATFORD CITY FROM 746 PM CDT TO 756 PM CDT MONDAY MAY 26 2014. PATH LENGTH 0.5 MILES PATH WIDTH 100 YARDS. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED 120 MPH. MORE DETAILS WERE SENT IN A PUBLIC INFORMATION STATEMENT.
TORNADO WAS REPORTED ALONG HIGHWAY 85 AT 748 PM CDT. MOVING SOUTHEAST.
OIL FIELD MAN CAMP 5 MILES SOUTH OF WATFORD CITY STRUCK BY TORNADO. NEAR MILE POST 136 ON U.S. HIGHWAY 85. ESTIMATED 15 TRAILERS SEVERELY DAMAGED. NO WORD ON INJURIES OR DEATHS.

SHERIFF FROM ASHLEY CALLED TO REPORT DAMAGE TO A STEEL STRUCTURE AND A WOODEN BUILDING. BOTH BUILDINGS WERE SIDE BY SIDE. THE STEEL STRUCTURE WAS BLOWEN OR LIFTED ABOUT 300 YARDS FROM ITS FOUNDATION. THE WOODED BUILDING WAS DAMAGED.

FOLLOW UP TO REPORTS FROM WEDNESDAY. THREE RVS IN TOBACCO GARDEN BAY SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE. ONE WAS TOPPLED. NO INJURIES. THERE WAS SPECULATION THAT THIS WAS A TORNADO. IT HAS BEEN DETERMINED THROUGH AN EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT STORM SURVEY THAT IT WAS NOT. IT WAS HIGH WIND.

*** 1 INJ *** A CAMPER WAS TIPPED ON ITS SIDE...HORSE TRAILER MOVED AND A QUONSET DAMAGED BY A POSSIBLE TORNADO. A NINE YEAR OLD CHILD WAS TRAPPED IN THE CAMPER PULL-OUT. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WILL BE SENT WHEN IT BECOMES AVAILABLE.

*** 1 INJ *** FOLLOW UP TO REPORTS FROM WEDNESDAY. ONE MINOR INJURY OF A NINE YEAR OLD FEMALE. ON A FARM...TREES SNAPPED...DAMAGE TO OUTBUILDINGS AND FENCES. INJURY IN A 5TH WHEEL THAT WAS TIPPED. HORSE TRAILER TIPPED. THERE WAS SPECULATION THAT THIS WAS A TORNADO. IT HAS BEEN DETERMINED THROUGH PHOTO EVIDENCE THAT IT WAS NOT A TORNADO. HIGH WIND.
